metadata.notes: Install-only landscape pricing. Excludes recurring maintenance and fence installs. All figures 2026 nationally-blended installed unless noted. Apply stateMultipliers OR regionMultipliers (not both) when localizing.

west_coast.notes: CA highest; drought premium for natives, smart irrigation pushed by code

southeast.notes: Year-round work, cheaper labor, cheap sod

texas.notes: Summer slowdown, cheap sod, expensive irrigation (TCEQ licensing)

zone3.notes: Short growing season; install size matters more

zone5.notes: Most national plant lists target zone 5-6

zone7.notes: Transition zone; fescue and bermuda both work

zone8.notes: Zoysia / centipede lawn; zoysia install ~30% more than fescue

zone9.notes: Bermuda, St. Augustine; irrigation costs spike

stamped_concrete_patio.label: Stamped concrete patio
stamped_concrete_patio.notes: Cracks within 5-10 years, hard to repair

scopeCheckItems.label: Drainage plan / slope away from house
Rain sensor / smart controller on irrigation
State license number listed (CA C-27, TX LI, OR LCB, NV C-10)
Permits addressed (walls >4 ft, tree ordinance, backflow)

redFlagPatterns.name: door_to_door_pitch
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Door-to-door pitches and 'leftover material' offers are the #1 vector for tree-service and driveway-sealing fraud per BBB and AARP. Legitimate landscapers do not canvass door-to-door, especially after storms.
redFlagPatterns.action: Never sign or pay same-day. Get the company name, check BBB and the state contractor license board.

redFlagPatterns.name: tree_topping
redFlagPatterns.explanation: ANSI A300 and ISA standards explicitly prohibit tree topping. It is arboricultural malpractice, kills the tree, creates hazard regrowth, and voids any reputable warranty.
redFlagPatterns.action: Disqualify the bidder. Hire an ISA Certified Arborist for proper crown reduction or removal.

redFlagPatterns.name: climbing_spikes_on_pruning
redFlagPatterns.explanation: ANSI A300 forbids spikes on pruning jobs. Spikes puncture the cambium and invite decay. Allowed only on removals.
redFlagPatterns.action: Reject spike use on any pruning job. Walk away if the bidder insists.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_isa_no_insurance_for_tree_work
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Uninsured climber falling on your property makes your homeowners insurance the payer. A real arborist will provide an ISA cert number and name you as additional insured.
redFlagPatterns.action: Demand ISA cert number and a COI naming you as additional insured before any work begins.

redFlagPatterns.name: cash_only_or_large_deposit
redFlagPatterns.explanation: BBB rule of thumb: deposits over ~33% are a red flag. CA caps residential deposits at 10% or $1,000, whichever is less. Cash leaves no chargeback trail.
redFlagPatterns.action: Refuse deposits over 33%. Pay by credit card or check made to a business, never to an individual.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_written_scope_no_cap
redFlagPatterns.requiresAbsenceOf: \b(not to exceed|fixed price|firm price|guaranteed price|line item)\b
redFlagPatterns.explanation: 70%+ of failed irrigation installs came from verbal-only 'estimates' with no line items. Final invoices routinely land 30-80% over the verbal quote.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require a written, line-itemed, 'not to exceed' quote before signing.

redFlagPatterns.name: sod_missing_prep_and_watering
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Sod laid on compacted, unprepped soil dies in weeks and voids the warranty. NALP best practice: 4-6" of tilled and amended soil + starter fert + first-30-days watering schedule in writing.
redFlagPatterns.action: Demand tilling depth, soil amendment, starter fertilizer, and a written 30-day watering schedule.

redFlagPatterns.name: sprinkler_missing_zones_backflow_sensor
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Without head-to-head spacing you get permanent brown spots. Without backflow you can contaminate the potable supply (code violation). Rain sensors are legally required in FL, NJ, MN, and parts of TX.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require zone count, head layout, backflow preventer, rain sensor, and (in cold climates) a winterization plan in writing.

redFlagPatterns.name: hardscape_missing_base_geotextile_edge
redFlagPatterns.explanation: ICPI Tech Spec 2: pedestrian patios need 4" compacted base minimum, driveways 6", cold climates +2-4". Missing edge restraint = pavers spread within one season. Missing geotextile over weak subgrade = pumping and settlement.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require base depth in inches, geotextile callout, edge restraint, compactor passes, and slope away from the house.

redFlagPatterns.name: retaining_wall_over4_no_engineering
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Almost every jurisdiction requires a stamped engineer drawing and a permit for walls >4 ft (some >3 ft). Failure = the wall blows out in 1-3 freeze cycles and the homeowner owns the liability.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require stamped engineered drawings, a building permit, geogrid, and a drainage detail.

redFlagPatterns.name: plant_warranty_fine_print
redFlagPatterns.explanation: NALP standard is 1-year on plant material with reasonable homeowner maintenance. Voids on 'any pest' or 'any drought' give the contractor a 100% out.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require a 1-year plant warranty in writing with reasonable-maintenance language, not open-ended voids.

redFlagPatterns.name: unlicensed_pesticide_application
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Every state requires a separate Commercial Pesticide Applicator license for chemical apps, independent of landscape install license. Unlicensed application is a misdemeanor and can harm kids, pets, and wells.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require the applicator license number on the quote. Verify with your state Department of Agriculture.

redFlagPatterns.name: free_design_ip_grab
redFlagPatterns.explanation: 'Free design with install' plus a clause that the documents remain the contractor's property locks you to one bidder. NALP recommends design fees be itemized and transferable.
redFlagPatterns.action: Strike the IP clause or pay a modest itemized design fee for transferable plans.

redFlagPatterns.name: sod_install_during_drought
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Sod install during Stage 2+ drought voids warranty and may violate local watering ordinances. SNWA (Las Vegas) bans non-functional turf by 2027. CA prohibits new non-functional turf at commercial sites.
redFlagPatterns.action: Verify your local watering stage and check cash-for-grass rebates before installing thirsty turf.

redFlagPatterns.name: uninsured_subcontracted_tree_work
redFlagPatterns.explanation: If the climber isn't named on the bidder's COI, you own the liability for any injury on your property.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require the sub's Certificate of Insurance naming you as additional insured, or disqualify the bidder.

redFlagPatterns.name: prepay_for_materials
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Classic disappearance scam. Reputable nurseries extend trade credit; the contractor should not need your money to order plants.
redFlagPatterns.action: Refuse to pre-pay for materials. Pay on delivery or completion milestones.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_permit_in_protected_district
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Atlanta, Seattle, Portland, Austin, Tampa, Sacramento, DC and others have tree-protection ordinances. Atlanta charges $500/inch DBH for unpermitted tree removal. Homeowner gets the fine, not the contractor.
redFlagPatterns.action: Verify tree ordinance and HOA rules before signing. Require the contractor to pull any required permits.

redFlagPatterns.name: cold_climate_no_winterization
redFlagPatterns.explanation: In cold-climate states, an irrigation system without an annual blow-out plan will freeze and crack within the first winter.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require a winterization plan in writing before installation.

redFlagPatterns.name: vague_licensed_and_insured
redFlagPatterns.explanation: 'Fully licensed and insured' with no actual license or policy number is unverifiable and frequently false.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require the state license number and policy number on the written quote.

redFlagPatterns.name: unrealistically_low_bid
redFlagPatterns.externalSignal: bid > 25% below median of peer bids on same scope
redFlagPatterns.explanation: A bid >25% below peers on the same scope is either missing scope (change orders will eat the savings), using uninsured labor, or substituting down a material grade.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ask the bidder to match the peer scope line-by-line in writing. If they can't or won't, skip them.

sod: Roll and water on install day
1-year warranty contingent on following watering schedule
irrigation: Zone count and head layout diagram
Backflow preventer (RPZ or PVB) per code
EPA WaterSense smart controller (Rachio 3, Hunter Hydrawise HC, Rain Bird ESP-TM2 LNK)
Rain sensor (required in FL, NJ, MN, parts of TX)
hardscape_paver: 4 inch compacted base minimum for patios (6 inch for driveways)
retaining_wall: Stamped engineered drawings for walls >4 ft
Drainage rock + perforated pipe behind wall
tree_work: COI naming homeowner as additional insured
No spikes on live tree pruning

questionsToAsk: What is your state license number and can I verify it with the state board?
Can you name me as additional insured on your COI for this job?
Is this a firm, not-to-exceed quote or an estimate that can change?
What is the deposit percentage and is it 33% or less?
For sod: what tilling depth, soil amendment, and starter fertilizer are included?
For irrigation: how many zones, what controller, is a backflow preventer and rain sensor included?
For pavers: what is the base depth, is geotextile and edge restraint included, and what slope is spec'd?
For retaining walls over 4 ft: do you have stamped engineering drawings and is a permit pulled?
For tree work: are you ISA certified, and will the climber use spikes on pruning?
For chemical applications: what is your pesticide applicator license number?
What plant warranty do you offer and what voids it?
Is design work transferable to me if I don't hire you for installation?
In a drought zone: are there local cash-for-grass or WaterSense rebates I qualify for?
Will the same company on this contract do the work, or is any of it subcontracted?
What is your committed start date and expected completion date?

droughtRebates.notes: LADWP tops at $5/sf. 3 plants per 100 sf, no invasives, no synthetic turf. Pre-approval required.
$3/sf first 10,000 sf, $1.50/sf above. Non-functional turf banned 2027. Pre-approval mandatory.
Varies by city. Mesa $0.50/sf. Tempe up to $1,500.

droughtRebates.program: Resource Central Garden in a Box
droughtRebates.notes: Pre-designed kits $130-170 subsidized ~$25-50; many utilities add $1-2/sf turf rebate

droughtRebates.program: Utah Water Savers / Slow the Flow

treePlantingRebates.program: SMUD Sacramento Shade Tree (w/ Sacramento Tree Foundation)
treePlantingRebates.benefit: Up to 10 free shade trees + planting consultation
Up to 7 free trees delivered and planted

CA.notes: Required for any job >$500 labor+material. Separate C-61/D-12 for synthetic turf. DPR QAL/QAC for any commercial pesticide app.

TX.notes: No statewide landscape license but irrigation requires TCEQ LI. Backflow tester separate license.

OR.license: OR LCB Landscape Contracting Business + Landscape Construction Professional

NV.license: NV State Contractors Board C-10 Landscape Contractor
NV.notes: SNWA also requires Water Smart Contractor cert for rebate eligibility in LV

AZ.notes: Bond required. AZ Dept of Ag QA/QP for pesticides.

LA.notes: Separate licenses for landscape architect, horticulturist, arborist, utility arborist. Landscape Horticulturist exam required.

FL.license: No statewide landscape license. Certified Pest Control Operator for chemical apps. County-level irrigation cert in Miami-Dade, Broward.
FL.notes: FFL BMP certification mandatory in many counties for fertilizer

GA.license: GA Landscape Contractor registration with state board
GA.notes: Atlanta tree ordinance - heritage tree fees

MD.license: MHIC for any home improvement + MD Tree Expert license for tree care
MD.notes: MD Tree Expert is one of the strictest in US

CT.license: HIC >$200 + CT Arborist license for tree work
CT.notes: CT Arborist is one of 4 statutory tree licenses in US

NJ.license: NJ HIC registration + NJ Certified Tree Expert for tree work

RI.license: RI Contractor Registration + RI Arborist license

MN.license: MN Tree Care Registration; MN Irrigation Contractor required

permitTriggers.trigger: Retaining wall >4 ft (measured from bottom of footing)
permitTriggers.notes: Some jurisdictions trigger at 3 ft if surcharged

permitTriggers.requires: Building, electrical, plumbing, barrier inspection; sometimes seismic

permitTriggers.trigger: Grading >50 cubic yards cut or fill

permitTriggers.requires: Tree removal permit + possibly mitigation fee

permitTriggers.trigger: Backflow preventer on new irrigation tied to municipal water
permitTriggers.requires: Required universally; annual testing by certified tester

permitTriggers.trigger: Pesticide/herbicide near schools, wells, wetlands, surface water
Fertilizer summer-N ban counties (FL: Pinellas, Sarasota, Manatee, Lee)
HOA architectural review (FL FFL plants preempted by statute 373.185)
Irrigation install permit (FL county, TX any, some CA cities, NC)

permitTriggers.requires: Electrical permit; dark sky compliance in AZ, NM, parts of UT, HI

permitTriggers.trigger: Erosion / sediment control for disturbance >1 acre (NPDES) or per state (DE/MD 5,000 sf)

permitTriggers.requires: State wetland permit (coastal + Great Lakes states)
No deep-rooted plantings within X ft; permit to alter
Mandatory pre-dig call; failure is criminal in many states

permitTriggers.trigger: Burn permit for vegetation disposal in fire-prone counties

permitTriggers.requires: Tree protection fencing at drip line
